sutta: the buddha said monks long ago a battle rage between the devas and the asuras and in that battle the asuras 1 and the devas were defeated and the defeated devas simply fled with the asuras facing north hot after them then the day was taught the asuras give chase let us battle with them a second time and a second time they fought and the second time were defeated and pursued and a third time they fought and the asuras won and the devas were defeated and defeated and fearful they just entered the deva city monks thus gone to the city the day was taught now that we have come to the refuge for the fearful we will henceforth dwell by ourselves and have no dealings with the asuras and the asuras thought also gone indeed other devas to the refuge for the fearful henceforth they will dwell by themselves and have no dealings with us monks long ago another battle raged between the devas and asuras but in that fight the devas won and the asuras were defeated and the defeated asuras fled pursued by the devas facing south then thought that asuras the devas pursue us what if we fight a second time and they did so and were a second time defeated and a third time they fought and the devas won and the asuras were defeated and defeated and fearful they just entered the asura city and thus gone to the city the asuras thought now that we've come to the refuge for the fearful we will dwell by ourselves and have nothing to do with the day of us and the devas thought likewise in just the same way monks what time a monk aloo from sen's desires enters and abides in the first jhana he thinks now that i have come to the refuge for the fearful i will henceforth dwell by myself and have no dealings with mara and mara the evil 1 thinks now that the monk has gone to the refuge for the fearful he will dwell by himself and have no dealings with me [Music] monks what time among enters and abides in the second jhana the third the fourth jannah he thinks likewise and mara 2 things likewise monks when a man enters and abides in the sphere of infinite space he is said to have put a darkness about nara and mara's vision being blotted out is without range and he has become invisible to the evil 1 monks when among enters and abides in the sphere of infinite consciousness in the sphere of nothingness in the sphere of neither perception or non-perception and by wisdom sees that the asavas are completely destroyed he is said to have put a darkness about mara and mara's vision being blotted out is rangeless and he has become invisible to the evil 1 and has passed through the world's entanglement just that's the end of the suta
commentary:
so here the buddha is giving a simile when the devas and the asuras fight and 1 of them is defeated they go into the city and hide there and because the city gates are locked the the enemies cannot come inside so they dwell in in their city which is called the refuge for the fearful so in the same way the buddha is saying when a man enters and abides in the first second third or fourth jana then he is beyond the reach of mara of satan why why is he beyond the reach of mara or satan because mara mara's world is the world of the 5 senses or you can if you can if you want to you can add the world of the 6 senses including thinking and as well uh if our 6 senses our sixth sense doors are open and we go out and we pay attention to forms especially beautiful forms beautiful sounds smells taste touch and thoughts then mara will tempt us and we can be caught or defeated by mara but when we go into the jaanas and the first second third and fourth jhana then that is out of the range of uh mahara because mara cannot enter the jaanas mara is unable to enter the jana jaanas mara can only bait us he can bait us with a beautiful form sound smells taste touch and even thoughts but when our mind is controlled when we are 1 pointed in jhana nothing can shape us mara is we is we are beyond the reach of mara that is why for example in the description of the first jana the buddha says aloo from sense desires aloo from unwholesome states among enters and abides in the first jhana ah which has uh which is accompanied by the light bt and sukkah pleasure um and so so in the description of the first jhana you can see here that the first jhana is a statement which is aloof from sen's desires and also alluve from unwholesome states aloo from unwholesome states that means uh it is a totally wholesome state janas are totally wholesome states there is nothing unwholesome about janus it is not a state of sloth and top not a trance state not a sleepy state it is a state of intense mindfulness that is why because in the state of the first gen the mind is in control of itself that is why it's beyond the reach of mahara but when a person enters the arupa janas and the spheres of infinite space infinite consciousness nothingness and neither perception or non-perception then the mind opens up opens up to infinite space infinite consciousness etc and when that happens mahara cannot see you you're beyond the vision of mahara that's why here the suta you have put a darkness about mara and mara's vision is blotted out so you see these jhanas are great states when you attain these jhana states we have gone beyond the reach of mara so that is the way to uh to attain liberation to be freed of mara by attaining the jaanas the nexus is 9.4.39 monks when bull or cow or young or baby elephant browsing on the grass stops forestalls some forest tusker about to take his feet so that he is played provoked and waxed when bull or cow or young or baby elephant devours the bunches of broken bowels when bull or cow or young fin stirring up the water with his trunk forestalls the forest tusker gone down to the water hole when cow elephants go and rub against the body of the tusker gone down to the waterhole so that he's plagued provoked and vexed 10 months the forest tusker thinks in faith i live now in a crowd of poor elephants cow elephants young elephants baby elephants i eat merely grass double they eat my bunches of broken boughs i drink muddy water and cow elephants come and rub against my body when i've gone to the waterhole what if i dwell alone withdrawn from the earth and presently monks he dwells alone withdrawn from the herd and he eats uncropped grass and none devours his bunches of bowels he drinks unmuddy water and cow elephants come not and rub against him in the water hole and at that time he thinks in the past i dwelled in a crowd but now i dwell alone withdrawn and breaking off a bunch of bowels with his trunk he rubs his body with it and joyfully allays the itch he stopped here for a moment here the buddha is talking about the forest tasker like the leader of the herd when he walks with his crowd of elephants and he wants to eat the grass he finds that the other elephants has eaten the grass so that the grass becomes very short for him to eat and he gets annoyed and then because he's bigner he breaks off the branches the twigs and the leaves to eat and when he breaks them off to the ground before he starts to eat the other other elephants come and eat up the the these bows that he has broken off the bunches of twigs and leaves etc so he gets annoyed with them again then when he wants to go and drink in the waterhole to drink the clear water all the other elephants go in and disturb the water and churn up the mud so that is not clean water that he gets to drink so he gets annoyed with them again when he goes down to the to the water to bathe the other elephants come along and push him here and there and he is annoyed again so he decides to go off on his own and dwell alone and this is what he does you see normally elephants they are in danger of being attacked by a bunch of lions for example so that is why they group together so that they get protection from each other but sometimes these big elephants are because they are annoyed with the crowd they wander off alone and because they are big and strong they are not afraid to wander off alone but not the young ones so the buddha continues and it is just so monks when a monk lives in a crowd of monks nuns lay disciples both men and women rajas their ministers course setters and their disciples and he thinks truly i now live in a crowd what if i were to live alone withdrawn from the horde and he resorts to some secluded dwelling forest tree root mountain glen cliff cave cemetery wooded upland open space or heap of straw and come to the forest tree root or empty hunt it sits cross-legged holds his body iraq and sets mindfulness before him and putting away a worldly coveting he abides with mind free therefrom he purges his mind of coveting putting away ill will and hatred he abides with mind free there from kindly compassionate towards all creatures he purges his mind of ill will and hatred putting away sloth in topper he abides free there from conscious alert mindful and aware he purges his minor sloth in topper putting away restlessness and worry he abides unflowed with mind inwardly calmed he purges his mind of restlessness and worry putting her way down he abides with doubt pass by know how how questioner he purges his mind of doubtness to right things and putting away these 5 hindrances weakening the mind's corruptions by wisdom aloof from sense desires aloof from unwholesome states he enters and abides in the first jhana joyous at having a lay the return suppressing applied and sustained thought he and enters and abides in the second jana then in the third jana fourth jana sphere of infinite space sea of infinite consciousness fear of nothingness fear of neither perception or non-perception in the ending of perception and feeling and seeing by wisdom that the asavas are completely destroyed and rejoices greatly at having the written as the end of the suta so here in the same way the buddha says when a monk lives in a crowd of people monks nuns lay disciples rajas ministers etc then he is irritated and he decides to go off and live alone in a cave or somewhere and living alone he purges his mind of the 5 hindrances sensual desire ill will sloth and topper restlessness and worry and doubt by practicing mindfulness and meditation and after that he attains the first jhana second jhana etc until the ending of perception and feeling and his asaphas are destroyed now by wisdom and then he has completely allayed the returns or the annoyances so that is the way and the buddha says that we should practice and that is why in the suit we find very often the buddha encourages a monk to go off and live alone because in a crowd of people is very difficult to practice there are a lot of irritants and things to annoy among whereas if a monk lives alone then it is much easier to attain the jana's states of deep meditation and from there to destroy the assabas and attain liberation
